results.title: 9 best GPT-5.6 alternatives in 2026 | eesel AI
results.content:
What's the best free alternative to GPT-5.6?

DeepSeek's web and app chat is free with no metered cap, and Meta AI is free across Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp with no subscription tier at all. For self-hosting, Qwen's open-weight Qwen3 line can run on your own hardware at zero API cost. See our Qwen pricing and DeepSeek overview for the details.

Is Claude or Gemini smarter than GPT-5.6?

Claude Fable 5 is still the model GPT-5.6 gets measured against; independent commentary on OpenAI's own system card called Sol 'a substantial improvement over GPT-5.5, but still short of Mythos.' Gemini 3.1 Pro trades blows with Sol on math and technical precision rather than beating it outright. Our Claude Fable 5 review and Gemini 3 pricing guide break down where each one actually wins. [...] Table of Contents

 Why people are looking for a GPT-5.6 alternative, even on launch day
 The 9 alternatives at a glance
 How I picked these
 1. Claude Fable 5 - best for raw intelligence
 2. Grok 4.5 - best for cheap agentic tool use
 3. Gemini 3.1 Pro - best Google-ecosystem all-rounder
 4. DeepSeek-V4 - best free and cheap open-weight pick
 5. Mistral Vibe - best for EU data residency
 6. Perplexity - best if you actually want a search engine
 7. Qwen - best for self-hosting and rock-bottom pricing
 8. Microsoft Copilot - best if you live in Microsoft 365
 9. Meta AI - best free pick if cutting-edge isn't the priority
 Does the underlying model even matter for support?
 Try eesel

## Why people are looking for a GPT-5.6 alternative, even on launch day [...] Where GPT-5.6 actually sits on price versus its closest frontier rivals, once you plot all four against each other

## 4. DeepSeek-V4 - best free and cheap open-weight pick

DeepSeek's free web chat interface

DeepSeek is the Chinese lab that put open-weight frontier models on the map, and its current flagship, DeepSeek-V4, is the sharpest budget answer to GPT-5.6 on this list. The consumer chat is completely free with no metered cap, and the weights are public on GitHub for anyone who wants to self-host.

Pricing: `deepseek-v4-pro` at $0.435 per 1M input tokens and $0.87 per 1M output, roughly a tenth of GPT-5.6 Sol's rate; `deepseek-v4-flash` is even cheaper at $0.14/$0.28.

GPT-5.6 (Generative Pre-trained Transformer 5.6) is a large language model (LLM) released by OpenAI on June 26, 2026. The initial release was limited; OpenAI later announced that GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna would launch publicly on July 9, 2026 at 10AM PDT, while preview access expanded globally.( It was released alongside GPT Live 1, formerly GPT-Bidi 1.(

GPT-5.6 comes in three versions, from least to most capable: Luna, Terra and Sol.(

## Features

[edit] [...] ## Features

[edit]

GPT-5.6 includes models named Sol, Terra, and Luna. Sol (88.8%) and Sol Ultra (91.9%) beat Claude Mythos 5 (88.0%) at TerminalBench 2.1, while Luna (82.5%) beat Claude Opus 4.8 (78.9%) although was below GPT-5.5 (83.4%), and Terra (84.3%) beat its own predecessor.( Terra is competitive with GPT-5.5 while being half the cost._[non-primary source needed_]

results.title: GPT-5.6 IS HERE! BEST AI Model Ever? Beats Fable ...
results.content: Open AI is officially back as today the company launched the entire GBT 5.6 family with Soul, Terra, and Luna now generally available across chat GBT codeex and the API following the earlier rollout restrictions. GBT 5.6 comes in three different tiers. Soul is built for long horizon coding and aentic work that requires planning, tool use, and reliable follow-through. Terra balances performance and cost for everyday professional workloads. Luna prioritizes speed and efficiency for well-defined high volume tasks. Across knowledge work evaluations, the GBT 5.6 Terra is reportedly surpassing GBT 5.5 while costing less. Whereas the GBT 5.6 Luna is nearly matching GPT 5.5's peak performance as well as being under half the estimated API costage. But the real headline is the GPT 5.6 Soul. OpenAI [...] Fable 5, artificial intelligence is also putting it down below Fable 5, but just by a slight margin. But what you can get out of that is that you do realize that Fable 5 is the best model, but you're paying it an incredible amount to get that usage. So, why not just use the GPT 5.6 six soul cuz it's just slightly worse but much cheaper than Fable 5. You're practically getting similar results and I bet if you are to put it into a loop, you can still get capable results similar to Fable 5 from the GBT 51.6 Soul. Now, the pricing is the same as the preview where it's basically listing Soul at $5 for 1 million input tokens and $30 per 1 million output tokens. And the context window right now for all three of these models is 1.5 million token context window, which is definitely remarkable. [...] costage. But the real headline is the GPT 5.6 Soul. OpenAI claims that Soul sets a new standard for intelligence across different domains as well as its efficiency in coding, knowledge work, cyber security, science, and agentic tasks while using fewer tokens and delivering results at lower cost. Performance-wise, the GBT 5.6 Soul is posting some seriously impressive numbers across agentic work, coding, computer use, cyber security, and research on agents/exam. PBC 5.6 6 Soul is reportedly reaching a 53.6 percentage, placing it 13.1 points ahead of Claude Fable 5 Adaptive. What makes the results even more impressive is the efficiency. At medium reasoning, it even still beats the Fable 5 with a substantial gain. And even Luna is reportedly outperforming Fable 5 adaptive at around 116th of

I could give you guys my opinion from testing it for the last, you know, 12 hours, but to be honest, that's not enough depth for the people that have been testing this early that have had exclusive OpenAI access. And them having it for 2 months certainly should say something. So, this guy, Mashima, essentially has had GPT-5.6 since May 27th, okay? And for the first 2 weeks, he said that this model was super impressive, but the TLDR is that Claude Fable is better than GPT-5.6 in certain ways. The benchmarks may look close, but apparently Fable has a big model smell. GPT-5.6 feels like a smaller model that has had reinforcement learning making it appear very well, and the difference that shows up the moment you push past normal coding work.
